---
format: html
title: assert_prediction_probabilities()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>assert_prediction_probabilities</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Assert Prediction Probabilities</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Validates that a prediction matrix or data frame contains finite
probability-scale values bounded by zero and one.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>assert_prediction_probabilities(data_predictions, tolerance = 1e-08)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data_predictions">data_predictions</code></td>
<td>
<p>Numeric matrix or data frame of predicted probabilities.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="tolerance">tolerance</code></td>
<td>
<p>Numeric scalar tolerance for small floating-point excursions beyond
zero and one.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>The input 'data_predictions', unchanged.
</p>


<h3>Examples</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>assert_prediction_probabilities(
  data_predictions = matrix(c(0.1, 0.9), nrow = 1)
)
</code></pre>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
